interactions.label: Which statement BEST captures the principle of 'selective toxicity' as the foundation of rational antimicrobial chemotherapy?

params.question: Which statement BEST captures the principle of 'selective toxicity' as the foundation of rational antimicrobial chemotherapy?

answers.text: The drug accumulates only in infected tissue and avoids healthy tissue entirely.

tipsAndFeedback.tip: Selective accumulation describes pharmacokinetic distribution, not selective toxicity. Most antimicrobials distribute widely to host tissues.

answers.text: The drug exploits structural or metabolic differences between pathogen and host to harm the pathogen preferentially.

tipsAndFeedback.tip: Correct. Paul Ehrlich's principle: targeting structures absent in humans (e.g., peptidoglycan, ergosterol) or sufficiently divergent (e.g., 70S ribosome, dihydropteroate synthase) gives a favourable therapeutic index.

answers.text: The drug is toxic only in selected patient populations such as the elderly or pregnant women.

tipsAndFeedback.tip: That describes population-specific adverse effects, not the core principle of selective toxicity.

answers.text: The drug kills only Gram-positive organisms while sparing Gram-negative organisms.

tipsAndFeedback.tip: Spectrum of activity is a separate concept. A narrow-spectrum drug can still violate selective toxicity if it harms host cells.

interactions.label: Mr. Suresh, 35, is newly diagnosed with smear-positive pulmonary tuberculosis at an Indian district hospital and is registered under NTEP. Which regimen will he receive during the 2-month intensive phase?

params.question: Mr. Suresh, 35, is newly diagnosed with smear-positive pulmonary tuberculosis at an Indian district hospital and is registered under NTEP. Which regimen will he receive during the 2-month intensive phase?

tipsAndFeedback.tip: HR alone is the continuation phase regimen (4 months). Using only two drugs in the intensive phase risks rapid resistance emergence in the high bacillary load of active disease.

answers.text: Isoniazid + Rifampicin + Pyrazinamide + Ethambutol (HRZE), daily, for 2 months

tipsAndFeedback.tip: Correct. NTEP (and WHO) prescribe daily fixed-dose combination HRZE in the intensive phase to rapidly reduce bacillary load and prevent resistance, followed by 4 months of HRE in the continuation phase.
BPaL is reserved for highly drug-resistant TB (pre-XDR/MDR-TB intolerant to fluoroquinolones), not drug-sensitive disease.

answers.text: Streptomycin + Isoniazid for 2 months, then Isoniazid alone for 7 months

tipsAndFeedback.tip: This resembles obsolete pre-rifampicin regimens. Modern short-course chemotherapy mandates rifampicin-containing four-drug induction.
